title: leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. minima
intro: Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. minima, also known as the Alpine Daisy or Alpine Chamomile, is a small, perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. minima include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for insomnia and restlessness. Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. minima can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
